perturbation.original_text: 1. Term. Unless earlier terminated, this Agreement shall take effect on December 1st, 2015 and shall expire on November 30, 2016. XLI and/or WCC may renegotiate and/or cancel this Agreement at any time during its term, in the event any of the following conditions occur: 1. Any significant changes to the Benefits Schedule which cannot be cured as stated in Section 2.2 (Termination upon Breach) 2. By mutual consent of WCC and XLI to pursue other arrangements. 3. In the event either party becomes bankrupt or insolvent.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Term. Unless earlier terminated, this Agreement shall take effect on December 1st, 2015 and shall expire on November 30, 2016. XLI may renegotiate and/or cancel this Agreement at any time during its term. WCC can only renegotiate and/or cancel this Agreement during its term, in the event any of the following conditions occur: 1. Any significant changes to the Benefits Schedule which cannot be cured as stated in Section 2.2 (Termination upon Breach) 2. By mutual consent of WCC and XLI to pursue other arrangements. 3. In the event either party becomes bankrupt or insolvent.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction by restricting WCC's ability to renegotiate or cancel the agreement to specific conditions, while granting XLI the freedom to do so at any time. This contradicts the original term that both parties had the same renegotiation rights and creates uncertainty about the specific conditions under which WCC can act. The clause that initially said both parties could renegotiate at anytime has now been constricted for just one of the parties. It is now not clear if WCC can renegotiate at anytime like XLI can.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Term. Unless earlier terminated, this Agreement shall take effect on December 1st, 2015 and shall expire on November 30, 2016. XLI may renegotiate and/or cancel this Agreement at any time during its term. WCC can only renegotiate and/or cancel this Agreement during its term, in the event any of the following conditions occur: 1. Any significant changes to the Benefits Schedule which cannot be cured as stated in Section 2.2 (Termination upon Breach) 2. By mutual consent of WCC and XLI to pursue other arrangements. 3. In the event either party becomes bankrupt or insolvent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text implies both XLI and WCC have equal rights to renegotiate or cancel the agreement at any time. The changed text explicitly restricts WCC's ability to renegotiate or cancel to specific conditions, while granting XLI the freedom to do so at any time. This creates an inconsistency regarding the conditions under which each party can act, directly conflicting with the initial understanding of equal renegotiation rights as presented in the original agreement. The contradiction arises because the amended clause directly limits WCC's rights while preserving those of XLI, diverging from the original provision.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Agreement Fee 3.1 In consideration of the rights herein granted, and in keeping with XLI's desire to utilize WCC in the PROMOTION of its products and technology, XLI will pay WCC in accordance with Attachment B, which shall be deemed a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Agreement Fee 3.1 In consideration of the rights herein granted, and in keeping with XLI's desire to utilize WCC in the PROMOTION of its products and technology, XLI will pay WCC in accordance with Attachment B, which shall be deemed a part of this Agreement. However, XLI's obligation to pay WCC is contingent upon XLI's sole determination that WCC has adequately fulfilled its promotional obligations.
perturbation.explanation: The added text introduces a contradiction by making XLI's payment obligation to WCC contingent on XLI's subjective assessment of WCC's performance. This conflicts with the initial clause stating that XLI *will* pay WCC as per Attachment B, suggesting an unconditional payment obligation. Now it creates uncertainty as to when payments will take place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Agreement Fee 3.1 In consideration of the rights herein granted, and in keeping with XLI's desire to utilize WCC in the PROMOTION of its products and technology, XLI will pay WCC in accordance with Attachment B, which shall be deemed a part of this Agreement. However, XLI's obligation to pay WCC is contingent upon XLI's sole determination that WCC has adequately fulfilled its promotional obligations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states XLI 'will pay' WCC according to Attachment B, suggesting a definite payment obligation. The changed text adds a contingency, making the payment dependent on XLI's subjective assessment of WCC's performance. This directly contradicts the original seemingly unconditional payment obligation, creating an inconsistency regarding the payment terms. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, specifically when 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 Approvals. All requests for "approval" hereunder shall be in writing (email) and shall provide the party from whom approval is sought a period of not less than ten (10) days in which to respond. All responses shall be in writing (email) and, in the instance where approval is denied, shall include an explanation for the denial of approval. In the absence of a written (email) response, a request for approval shall be deemed denied. In those instances in which a party has been granted "discretion" hereunder, such right may be exercised in the sole and absolute discretion of the party having such right.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 Approvals. All requests for "approval" hereunder shall be in writing (email) and shall provide the party from whom approval is sought a period of not less than ten (10) days in which to respond, unless otherwise specified in the relevant section of this Agreement. All responses shall be in writing (email) and, in the instance where approval is denied, shall include an explanation for the denial of approval, except in cases where the approval concerns confidential information. In the absence of a written (email) response, a request for approval shall be deemed approved. In those instances in which a party has been granted "discretion" hereunder, such right may be exercised in the sole and absolute discretion of the party having such right, without any obligation to provide justification.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces in-text contradictions to the approval process. First, it allows for response times shorter than ten days if another section specifies it, creating inconsistency. Second, it removes the requirement for explanation when denying approval for confidential information, conflicting with the general rule. Third, it changes the default of no response from 'denied' to 'approved.' Finally, it states that party who granted “discretion” hereunder doesn’t need to give justification when it goes against the normal approval protocols.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Approvals. All requests for "approval" hereunder shall be in writing (email) and shall provide the party from whom approval is sought a period of not less than ten (10) days in which to respond, unless otherwise specified in the relevant section of this Agreement. All responses shall be in writing (email) and, in the instance where approval is denied, shall include an explanation for the denial of approval, except in cases where the approval concerns confidential information. In the absence of a written (email) response, a request for approval shall be deemed approved. In those instances in which a party has been granted "discretion" hereunder, such right may be exercised in the sole and absolute discretion of the party having such right, without any obligation to provide justification.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces several in-text contradictions. First, it creates an exception to the 'not less than ten (10) days' response period, contradicting the original agreement's standardized approval timeline. Second, it allows for denying approval without explanation for confidential information, undermining the original requirement for explanation in all denial cases. Third, the default of 'deemed denied' is switched to 'deemed approved' upon no response. These changes, especially the shift from 'deemed denied' to 'deemed approved' and the introduction of exceptions to the explanation requirement, create a direct conflict with the consistent and standardized approval process that was initially outlined in the document and appears in both the original text and the contradicted text, leading to uncertainty about which terms should be enforced, satisfying the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. Finally, there is contradiction regarding the justification of “discretion”.
